addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1650:static void AddLZ77Data(const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
1875:    AddLZ77Data(lz77, lstart, lend, expected_data_size,

toolUseResult.stdout:
1650:static void AddLZ77Data(const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
1875:    AddLZ77Data(lz77, lstart, lend, expected_data_size,

content.content:
1650	static void AddLZ77Data(const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
1651	                        size_t lstart, size_t lend,
1652	                        size_t expected_data_size,
1653	                        const unsigned *ll_symbols, const unsigned *ll_lengths,
1654	                        const unsigned *d_symbols, const unsigned *d_lengths,
1655	                        unsigned char *bp,
1656	                        unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)
1657	/* The store and the two parallel symbol arrays it is iterated over are valid
1658	   for every index the loop touches, i.e. [lstart, lend). */
1659	__CPROVER_requires(lstart <= lend)
1660	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
1661	__CPROVER_requires(
1662	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lend * sizeof(*lz77->litlens)))
1663	__CPROVER_requires(
1664	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lend * sizeof(*lz77->dists)))
1665	/* The four DEFLATE code tables span the full litlen / dist alphabets; the loop
1666	   indexes them with raw literals (< 256), length symbols (<= 285) and distance
1667	   symbols (<= 29), all within these bounds. */
1668	__CPROVER_requires(
1669	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_symbols, (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_symbols)))
1670	__CPROVER_requires(
1671	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_lengths)))
1672	__CPROVER_requires(
1673	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(d_symbols, (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_symbols)))
1674	__CPROVER_requires(
1675	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_lengths)))
1676	/* Every code length actually used must be a non-zero Huffman length (the asserts
1677	   in the body) that fits in the 7-bit field the AddHuffmanBits contract allows.
1678	   Constraining the whole tables is a sound over-approximation. */
1679	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1680	    size_t kll; (kll < (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)
1681	                  ==> (ll_lengths[kll] >= 1 && ll_lengths[kll] <= 7)
1682	})
1683	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1684	    size_t kd; (kd < (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_D)
1685	                  ==> (d_lengths[kd] >= 1 && d_lengths[kd] <= 7)
1686	})
1687	/* Per-element validity of the LZ77 commands the loop will process:
1688	   - a literal (dist == 0) is a byte value < 256;
1689	   - a back-reference has a DEFLATE length in [3, 258] and a distance in
1690	     [1, 512].  The upper distance bound keeps ZopfliGetDistExtraBits(dist)
1691	     at most 7, which is the field width the AddBits contract permits. */
1692	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1693	    size_t il; (lstart <= il && il < lend && lz77->dists[il] == 0)
1694	                  ==> (lz77->litlens[il] < 256)
1695	})
1696	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1697	    size_t ir; (lstart <= ir && ir < lend && lz77->dists[ir] != 0)
1698	                  ==> (lz77->litlens[ir] >= 3 && lz77->litlens[ir] <= 258
1699	                       && lz77->dists[ir] >= 1 && lz77->dists[ir] <= 512)
1700	})
1701	/* The uncompressed-size cross-check is disabled, as documented above. */
1702	__CPROVER_requires(expected_data_size == 0)
1703	/* The bit-output cursor and the dynamic output array are valid single objects,
1704	   and the append macro starts on its no-reallocation path. */
1705	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)))
1706	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
1707	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
1708	__CPROVER_requires(*bp <= 7)
1709	__CPROVER_requires(((*outsize) & ((*outsize) - 1)) != 0)
1710	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, (*outsize) + 1))
1711	__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, *outsize, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))
1712	/* The bit cursor remains a valid in-byte index. */
1713	__CPROVER_ensures(*bp <= 7)
1714	{
1715	    size_t testlength = 0;
1716	    size_t i;
1717	
1718	    for (i = lstart; i < lend; i++)
1719	    {
1720	        unsigned dist = lz77->dists[i];
1721	        unsigned litlen = lz77->litlens[i];
1722	        if (dist == 0)
1723	        {
1724	            assert(litlen < 256);
1725	            assert(ll_lengths[litlen] > 0);
1726	            AddHuffmanBits(ll_symbols[litlen], ll_lengths[litlen], bp, out, outsize);
1727	            testlength++;
1728	        }
1729	        else
1730	        {
1731	            unsigned lls = ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(litlen);
1732	            unsigned ds = ZopfliGetDistSymbol(dist);
1733	            assert(litlen >= 3 && litlen <= 288);
1734	            assert(ll_lengths[lls] > 0);
1735	            assert(d_lengths[ds] > 0);
1736	            AddHuffmanBits(ll_symbols[lls], ll_lengths[lls], bp, out, outsize);
1737	            AddBits(ZopfliGetLengthExtraBitsValue(litlen),
1738	                    ZopfliGetLengthExtraBits(litlen),
1739	                    bp, out, outsize);
1740	            AddHuffmanBits(d_symbols[ds], d_lengths[ds], bp, out, outsize);
1741	            AddBits(ZopfliGetDistExtraBitsValue(dist),
1742	                    ZopfliGetDistExtraBits(dist),
1743	                    bp, out, outsize);
1744	            testlength += litlen;
1745	        }
1746	    }
1747	    assert(expected_data_size == 0 || testlength == expected_data_size);
1748	}
1749	
1750	static void AddDynamicTree(const unsigned *ll_lengths,
1751	                           const unsigned *d_lengths,
1752	                           unsigned char *bp,
1753	                           unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)
1754	/* The two length tables hold the full DEFLATE litlen / dist alphabets; EncodeTree
1755	   scans both alphabets in their entirety. */
1756	__CPROVER_requires(
1757	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_lengths)))
1758	__CPROVER_requires(
1759	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_lengths)))
1760	/* Each entry is a Huffman code length, which DEFLATE bounds by 15; EncodeTree uses
1761	   it as an index into its 19-entry code-length-code count table. */
1762	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1763	    size_t kl; (kl < (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==> (ll_lengths[kl] <= 15)
1764	})
1765	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
1766	    size_t kd; (kd < (size_t)ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==> (d_lengths[kd] <= 15)
1767	})
1768	/* The bit-output cursor and the dynamic output array are valid, single objects. */
1769	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)))
1770	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
1771	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
1772	/* *bp is a bit index within a byte, used as a shift amount. */
1773	__CPROVER_requires(*bp <= 7)
1774	/* Keep the append macro on its no-reallocation path for the first emitted byte. */
1775	__CPROVER_requires(((*outsize) & ((*outsize) - 1)) != 0)
1776	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, (*outsize) + 1))
1777	/* The function emits the tree encoding into the output buffer, advancing the
1778	   bit cursor and growing the buffer. */
1779	__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, *outsize, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))

content.text: The function already has a detailed specification. Let me check the callees' contracts and then run verification.

content.content:
755	static void AddHuffmanBits(unsigned symbol, unsigned length,
756	                           unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out,
757	                           size_t *outsize)
758	/* bp, out, outsize must be valid pointers. */
759	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)))
760	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
761	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
762	/* *bp is a bit index within a byte, used as a shift amount. */
763	__CPROVER_requires(*bp <= 7)
764	/* Every caller emits a single Huffman code whose length is at most 7 bits here,
765	   so the bit cursor wraps to 0 at most once and thus at most a single fresh byte
766	   is appended over the whole loop. */
767	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 7)
768	/* The buffer holds at least one byte that can be updated, plus room to append
769	   one more byte without overflowing.  Constraining *outsize to a non-power-of-
770	   two (and non-zero) value keeps the ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA macro on its
771	   no-reallocation path for that single append. */
772	__CPROVER_requires(((*outsize) & ((*outsize) - 1)) != 0)
773	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, (*outsize) + 1))
774	__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, *outsize, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))
775	/* One fresh byte is appended for each time the bit cursor lands on 0 while
776	   emitting the `length` bits. */
777	__CPROVER_ensures(*outsize ==
778	                  __CPROVER_old(*outsize)
779	                      + ((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length + 7) / 8
780	                         - (__CPROVER_old(*bp) + 7) / 8))
781	/* The bit cursor advances by `length`, modulo 8. */
782	__CPROVER_ensures(*bp == ((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length) & 7))
783	{
784	    /* TODO(lode): make more efficient (add more bits at once). */
785	    unsigned i;
786	    for (i = 0; i < length; i++)
787	    {
788	        unsigned bit = (symbol >> (length - i - 1)) & 1;
789	        if (*bp == 0)
790	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(0, out, outsize);
791	        (*out)[*outsize - 1] |= bit << *bp;
792	        *bp = (*bp + 1) & 7;
793	    }
794	}
795	
796	static void AddBits(unsigned symbol, unsigned length,
797	                    unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)
798	/* bp, out, outsize must be valid pointers. */
799	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)))
800	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
801	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
802	/* *bp is a bit index within a byte, used as a shift amount. */
803	__CPROVER_requires(*bp <= 7)
804	/* Every caller writes at most a 7-bit field (DEFLATE extra bits / fixed-width
805	   header fields), so the bit cursor wraps to 0 at most once and thus at most a
806	   single fresh byte is appended over the whole loop. */
807	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 7)
808	/* The buffer holds at least one byte that can be updated, plus room to append
809	   one more byte without overflowing.  Constraining *outsize to a non-power-of-
810	   two (and non-zero) value keeps the ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA macro on its
811	   no-reallocation path for that single append. */
812	__CPROVER_requires(((*outsize) & ((*outsize) - 1)) != 0)
813	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, (*outsize) + 1))
814	__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, *outsize, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))
815	/* One fresh byte is appended for each time the bit cursor lands on 0 while
816	   emitting the `length` bits. */
817	__CPROVER_ensures(*outsize ==
818	                  __CPROVER_old(*outsize)
819	                      + ((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length + 7) / 8
820	                         - (__CPROVER_old(*bp) + 7) / 8))
821	/* The bit cursor advances by `length`, modulo 8. */
822	__CPROVER_ensures(*bp == ((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length) & 7))
823	{
824	    /* TODO(lode): make more efficient (add more bits at once). */
825	    unsigned i;
826	    for (i = 0; i < length; i++)
827	    {
828	        unsigned bit = (symbol >> i) & 1;
829	        if (*bp == 0)
830	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(0, out, outsize);
831	        (*out)[*outsize - 1] |= bit << *bp;
832	        *bp = (*bp + 1) & 7;
833	    }
834	}
835	
836	/* The DEFLATE format limits Huffman code lengths to at most 15 bits, so the
837	   per-length count and base-code tables never need more than this many entries.
838	   This bounds the structural unrolling of the preconditions below. */
839	#define LENGTHSTOSYMBOLS_MAXBITS 15
840	
841	/*
842	Converts a series of Huffman tree bitlengths, to the bit values of the symbols.
843	*/
844	void ZopfliLengthsToSymbols(const unsigned *lengths, size_t n, unsigned maxbits,

input.command: cd /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code && timeout 1800 run-cbmc --function AddLZ77Data --file zopfli.c 2>&1 | tail -50

content.text:
CBMC reports `AddLZ77Data verified successfully`. The function already carried a complete set of contracts, and verification passes.

Let me confirm this isn't a fluke by checking the tool actually exercised the body (not a trivial/vacuous pass) — the requires include consistent, satisfiable constraints (`*bp <= 7`, non-power-of-two `*outsize`, fresh buffers, per-element LZ77 validity), and the callee contracts for `AddHuffmanBits`/`AddBits` are present and compatible.

## Summary

`AddLZ77Data` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` **verifies successfully** with CBMC.

The function was already fully specified:
- **Memory validity**: `lz77` struct and its `litlens`/`dists` arrays fresh over `[lstart, lend)`; the four DEFLATE code tables (`ll_symbols`, `ll_lengths`, `d_symbols`, `d_lengths`) fresh over the full litlen/dist alphabets.
- **Value bounds**: code lengths constrained to `[1, 7]` (the field width the `AddBits`/`AddHuffmanBits` contracts permit), and per-element LZ77 command validity — literals `< 256`, back-references with length `[3, 258]` and distance `[1, 512]` (keeping extra-bits ≤ 7).
- **Output cursor/buffer**: `*bp <= 7`, non-power-of-two `*outsize` (keeps the append macro on its no-realloc path), and `*out` fresh of size `*outsize + 1`.
- **Postcondition**: `*bp <= 7`.

The callee contracts (`AddHuffmanBits`, `AddBits`) it relies on are in place and consistent with these requirements. No changes were needed — the task was verification, and CBMC confirms it.
